source_strategy: use a separate survey or benchmark with explicit subgroup tables and minimum-base considerations

population_definition.target_population: respondents with multiple analyzable subgroup dimensions and measurable subgroup error concentration
population_definition.sample_design: development-only subgroup benchmark combining WVS-backed persona-reliability evaluation and a professional climate survey
population_definition.unit_of_analysis: subgroup outcome table plus respondent-item reliability slice
population_definition.notes: Used to test suppression rules, subgroup denominators, subgroup ranking fidelity, and disproportionate subgroup distortion.

reported_findings_template: which subgroup accumulates the most model distortion

human_reference_summary.secondary_source_paper: Assessing the Reliability of Persona-Conditioned LLMs as Synthetic Survey Respondents
human_reference_summary.reported_sample_or_scale: 861 respondents in the EEA survey plus more than 70K respondent-item instances in the WVS-backed reliability study
key_results: women, ethnic minorities, LGBTQ+ respondents, and people with disabilities report worse climate outcomes
subgroup disparities are large on satisfaction, inclusion, respect, discrimination, and harassment
persona prompting can redistribute error and undermine subgroup fidelity even when aggregate alignment looks acceptable
